programmes.show_description: To A Man With A Hammer, Everything Looks Like A Nail: Ah Sahm, Young Jun and Hong head to a border town owned by the host of a lucrative fight tournament. (S2, ep 6)
Series shining a new light on modern policing in Manchester and the challenges faced by officers as they come face to face with a range of criminal and social issues. (S1, ep 10)
Devon/Cornwall: Adrenaline-fuelled documentary following Britain's police as they wage war against crime. This week's fix focuses on police in Devon and Cornwall. (S1, ep 3)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S11, ep 3)

In New York City, a laptop belonging to a Caribbean traveller does not compute and a young Brit arrives at Detroit Metro Airport to visit an American cyber pal. (S1, ep 16)
In Port Huron, a strong odour leads to a secondary for two Americans and an officer discovers gold at Chicago O'Hare Airport. (S1, ep 17)

Airside at JFK Airport, officers rush to inspect some high-maintenance passengers and a mother and son are suspected of money structuring in Detroit. (S1, ep 7)
An inside smuggling job is suspected at JFK Airport. Food couriers could be carriers and in Detroit, a wrong turn separates some buds. (S1, ep 8)

The Siege: While Sisko leads a daring last stand against the Bajoran take-over forces, Kira and Dax embark on a desperate mission to reveal the truth about the coup.
Nemesis: SG-1 face their most formidable foe yet, a creature of mass destruction that not even the Asgard can control. (S3, ep 22)
Lineage: Tom and B'Elanna are overjoyed to learn that they are going to have a baby, but the latter's concerns soon start to grow. (S7, ep 12)
Haven: Counsellor Deanna Troi is caught between her feelings for Riker and her devotion to family customs when she faces a pre-arranged marriage.
Oh, Brother: Giant ant-like creatures from the future come through an anomaly. Abby's brother Jack ends up stranded in the future and the team go in to rescue him. (S3, ep 8)
Day of Death: The team try to get Rosalind to help as they frantically search for Officer Chen. Officer Lopez finds Wesley in a bad way. (S2, ep 11)
Now and Then: Harper takes Lucy under her wing as she returns to duty after her abduction. Nolan invites his son's fiance on a ride-along. (S2, ep 12)
Where the Wild Things Were: As Eve travels through the mirror to take on a mysterious foe, Nick attempts to follow her. (S6, ep 11)
If You Wait By The River Long Enough: Ah Sahm, Hong and Young Jun are held to account for their secret mission. A shocking scene rocks the Blake household. (S2, ep 7)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Wales: From violent drunks and drug dealers to a dangerous domestic incident, this week's fix focuses on cops based in West Wales. (S1, ep 4)

CBP officers question a Mexican national with odd travel plans and in Port Huron, a traveller's past may come back to haunt him. (S1, ep 18)
An officer suspects a US resident returning from the Caribbean may be hiding something and two construction workers wind up at the border by mistake. (S1, ep 19)

A frequent visitor of drug source countries is questioned in New York and a Canadian cowboy may be a mule. (S1, ep 9)
A bus passenger presents curious concoctions at JFK Airport and a Chicago canine sniffs out a stash of cash. (S1, ep 10)

Invasive Procedures: The crew must fight for Jadzia's life when a desperate Trill takes the group hostage and steals the Dax symbiont.
Small Victories: SG-1 must face and destroy the replicators that have made their way to Earth. (S4, ep 1)
Repentance: The crew members re-evaluate their ethical beliefs, as well as those of an alien criminal justice system, when they rescue a Nygean prison warden and his prisoners. (S7, ep 13)
Where No One Has Gone Before: The Enterprise travel into unknown territory more than a billion light years away when a test on the ship's propulsion system backfires.
Herd Logic: A herd of rhinoceros-like creatures come through an anomaly. The team arrive at the scene, except for Danny who is busy infiltrating Johnson's HQ. (S3, ep 9)
The Breaking Point: Having held off German attempts to overrun Bastogne, Easy Company now faces the task of taking the nearby town of Foy from the enemy. Contains flashing images. (7 of 10)
The Patrol: With the war winding down, Easy Co moves into an Alsatian town near the German border where they sleep just across the river from the Nazis. Contains flashing images. (8 of 10)
Zerstorer Shrugged: Nick goes back to his routes in search of answers as the Zerstorer arrives in Portland with a trail of bodies in its wake. (S6, ep 12)
